results.title: Clickers club: photo adventures in curriculum
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Tony Paice and Corporate Development, Western Region and TV ED Productions
results.brief_description: Outlines the aims of a new photography club established throughout Victorian schools. Marg Sheehan interviews Leigh Hepburn who introduces the Project Kit, Roxanne Rodgers who explains its uses in an integrated curriculum and Gail Evans who applies the resources during a primary school class excursion to the zoo.

results.title: Remembering Janefield ...
results.creator_credit: Eastern Energy and Land Use Team, State of Victoria, Department of Human Services and Tribal Communications
results.brief_description: Provides a brief history of the Janefield Training Centre, Bundoora with both ex-residents and ex-staff members sharing anecdotes and moving reflections on their experiences and years at the institution.

results.title: Melbourne: Australia's garden city
results.brief_description: This panoramic tour of Victoria’s capital provides the viewer with a vibrant snapshot of Melbourne and its surroundings. Highlights of the tour include: a trip on Melbourne’s famous trams, beautiful parks and gardens, historic landmarks Como House and Werribee Park, Cook’s Cottage in the scenic Fitzroy gardens, Old Melbourne Gaol, the Dandenongs and Puffing Billy, Marysville, Sovereign Hill, Ballarat and surrounds, the National Wool Museum at Geelong, Phillip Island and the tranquil bush surroundings of Healesville Sanctuary.

results.title: Fair treatment
results.creator_credit: Tim Johnson, Chris McGill and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: This program examines some of the most important issues facing people living with HIV and contains interviews with people who are HIV positive. It focuses on the availability of drugs for HIV patients, and the lack of action and apathy shown by Australian governments. It also explores the outlook on life of the interviewees, how the stigma of AIDS, the death of friends and loved ones has affected them and finally examines their attitudes to death and dying.

results.title: Carnivore capers
results.creator_credit: Mark Laughlin, Tony Paice and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: This program set in the Royal Melbourne Zoo looks at three types of carnivores - lions, cape hunting dogs and seals, and follows the keeper through a typical day.

results.title: Denial
results.brief_description: When you’re fourteen and in trouble, confusion builds on confusion. Kane’s secret has become too complicated, too painful, too dangerous, and he can’t begin to stop denying. This award-winning and internationally acclaimed film is a study of some of the difficulties affecting all youth growing up in the 90’s.

results.title: A Look inside
results.creator_credit: Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Shows different aspects of work inside the Australian National Animal Health Laboratory. After a brief introduction to the importance of the livestock industry and the effects of disease, viewers are taken inside the laboratory to see some of the microbiological features.

results.title: Rise
results.brief_description: ‘Rise’ is a revealing look at the lives contained within a Melbourne Housing Commission estate. Set against the backdrop of a high-rise building, this is a visual account of a largely unknown environment: as the camera moves through the building, a series of portraits unfolds, telling the stories of people from a wide range of cultural backgrounds. In the context of high density living, ‘Rise’ is a celebration of the individual.

results.title: Countering sexism
results.creator_credit: Don Fitzsimmons, Julie Sheehan, Sabina Baltrukomis and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: A compilation tape of four programmes addressing various aspects of sex discrimination. The episodes highlight women working in the media, the importance of gender inclusive curriculum, girls and science and sexual harrassment in schools.

results.title: Greek curriculum
results.creator_credit: Dimitri Man and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: The Greek Curriculum program is designed for children in Australian schools catering for the mother tongue and second language learners. The same program is repeated twice. The first has English subtitles and Greek dialogue whilst the second is in Greek only.
